rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. PROPRTY SUBJECT TO DISPOSITION $1567.00 U S et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers To Petitioner'S First Set Of Demand For Production And Inspection Of Documents; Petitioner'S First Set Of Special Interrogatories; Judicial Council Form Interrogatories (Proposed Order)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 29, 2014, Line 1 - PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA's Motion To Compel Answers To Petitioner'S First Set Of Demand For Production And Inspection Of Documents; Petitioner'S First Set Of Special Interrogatories. Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. No opposition filed. Respondent to provide answers and documents within 20 days of the date when the order is served by mail. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rkane1089@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (D302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ROY MILLER et al VS. MARCUS DANIEL MERCHASIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories By Pltf And Req. For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $4175.00 Against Pltf And His Attorney Rey Hassan Jointly And Severally
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 29, 2014, Line 5 - DEFENDANTs WILLIAM HEDDEN, MARCUS MERCHASIN and CONSOLIDATED ADJUSTING, INC.'s Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories By Pltf And Req. For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $4175.00 Against Plaintiff And His Attorney Rey Hassan Jointly And Severally. Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear to discuss foregoing. Although Plaintiff or his attorney should provide verification, the lack of verification would not prevent the interrogatory responses from being used as evidence against the Plaintiff. With respect to the motion to compel further answers to specific interrogatories: (a) interrogatories 6.4, 6.5, Plaintiff's counsel to explain why he does not have bills for current treatment and medication from Medi-Cal/Medicare, and if so, why he cannot provide this information; (b) interrogatory 6.7, Plaintiff's counsel to clarify which of the health care providers listed in response to interrogatory 6.4 provided advise to extent he can remember; (c) interrogatory 7.1, parties to address why any further response to this interrogatory is relevant to pending malicious prosecution action; (d) interrogatories 8.4, 8.5, 8.7, 8.8, in light of answer to interrogatory 8.8, the responses to interrogatories 8.4, 8.5 and 8.7 should be clarified; (e) interrogatories 12.4 and 12.5, parties to address why any further responses to this interrogatory are relevant to pending malicious prosecution action. Parties to address why any sanctions should be awarded and if so in what amount. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MARIA DE LA LUZ BELMONTE VS. BAY AREA PROPERTIES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 29, 2014, Line 4, CROSS COMPLAINANTS BAY AREA PROPERTIES LTD., ASM INVESTMENTS, INC., LELAH ZELINSKY, DAVID KAPLAN AND BETTY KAPLAN AS TRUSTEES' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Issues. Motion granted. There are no triable issues of fact as to whether Cross-Defendants owed Cross-Complainants an immediate duty to defend Cross-Complainants against the claims asserted by Plaintiffs in the underlying action. Further, no triable issues exist as to whether Cross-Defendants breached this contractual duty by failing to assume the defense of Cross-Complainants in the underlying action. Finally, Cross-Complainants are entitled to the reasonable costs and attorneys' fees incurred in defending the underlying action from the date that the defense was first tendered. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 29, 2014, Line 8 - PLAINTIFF ANDERSON, ROWE & BUCKLEY INC.'s Motion To Compel Defendant Ajax Boiler, Inc.S Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set 1; Special Interrogatories, Set 1; Requests For Production Of Documents, Set 1; Request For Admissions, Set 1 And For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear after meeting and conferring to determine procedure due to supplemental responses to discovery. As the court is unaware as to what was responded to, it cannot determine what is still at issue. To the extent that defendant is claiming that all documents have been produced despite objections, court is prepared to make an order confirming the foregoing so as to prevent any surprise at trial. To the extent plaintiff is claiming that documents were not produced due to relevance objections, court is prepared to hear those issues. Court is also prepared to address issues with respect to form and special interrogatories in light of Coito v. Sup. Ct. 54 Cal.4th 480, as well as requests for admission in light of CCP §2033.010. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JACK K FRAZIER VS. MICHAEL QUINN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 29, 2014, Line 11, DEFENDANT MICHAEL QUINN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied. Defendant met his initial burden by introducing evidence that he was driving a San Francisco fire truck in response to an emergency call at the time of the accident, which meets the criteria for immunity under Cal. Veh. Code sec. 17004. (Defendant's Undisputed Material Facts 1-5.) Plaintiff's evidence raises a triable issue of material fact as to whether Defendant was intoxicated at the time of the accident. (Plaintiff's Additional Material Facts 32, 44; Declaration of Naresh Jain, Ph.D, pars. 13-17.) The court's task "in construing a statute is to ascertain the intent of the Legislature so as to effectuate the purpose of the law." (Dyna-Med, Inc. v. Fair Employment & Housing Com. (1987) 43 Cal.3d 1379, 1386.) "Both the legislative history of the statute and the wider historical circumstances of its enactment may be considered in ascertaining the legislative intent." (Id. at 1387.) The plain language of the statute makes clear that the Legislature's intent was to have public employees feel secure in carrying out their assigned duties, by eliminating the risk that they would be held personally liable if an accident were to occur. This policy would not be furthered by a grant of immunity in this instance, where there is a triable issue as to whether Defendant became willfully intoxicated and drove, giving rise to a "demonstrable and almost inevitable risk visited upon the innocent public by his voluntary conduct." (Taylor v. Sup. Ct. (1979) 24 Cal.3d 890, 897-98.) Defendant's objections 1-4 and 6 are sustained. Defendant's objections 5 and 7 are overruled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD MORGAN AKA LUCIAN JAY REITANO VS. ESTATE OF PETER GEORGE EUTENEUER
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ; Supporting Evidence
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 29, 2014, Line 14, DEFENDANT ESTATE OF PETER GEORGE EUTENEUER, BY AND THROUGH MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The court grants the motion in part. The court grants summary judgment in favor of defendant against plaintiff's complaint. First, the undisputed evidence demonstrates that Mr. Holmes assigned the promissory note to plaintiff and plaintiff was a co-obligor on the note. (Defendant's UMF 4). "It has long been established in California that the assignment of a joint and several debt to one of the co-obligors extinguishes that debt. [citations]. The assignment amounts to payment and consequently the evidence of that debt, i.e., the note or judgment, becomes functus officio (of no further effect). [citations] Therefore, no action can be maintained on the original debt." Great Western Bank v. Kong (2001) 90 Cal.App.4th 28, 32. Second, plaintiff failed to provide an opposing separate statement that complies with CCP 437c(b)(3). The court denies summary judgment on the cross-complaint because defendant failed to maintain its initial burden of production. The motion does not address all of the claims in the cross-complaint, appears to be directed only at the cause of action for cancellation of the instrument, and defendant did not seek summary adjudication in the alternative. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: HEATHER RODRIGUES et al VS. PRINCIPAL BUILDERS INC A CALIFORNIOA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Trifurcate Plaintiffs' Cases Into Se4parate Trials And Segregate Trial On Issues Of Liability From Punitive Damages
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 29, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANT PRINCIPAL BUILDERS INC., SCOTT REAY, JON-MICHAEL JOHNSON'S Motion To Trifurcate Plaintiffs' Cases Into Separate Trials And Segregate Trial On Issues Of Liability From Punitive Damages. The motion is denied. There is a sufficient nexus between the issues to be heard in the same trial. CC 3295 requires evidence of defendant's financial condition to be heard in a separate proceeding. Plaintiffs' request to bifurcate the complaint and cross-complaint in the opposition is procedurally improper. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
